authoritative_sources.title: 36 CFR Part 1222 - Creation and Maintenance of Federal Records
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Archives and Records Administration / U.S. Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA is the federal records authority, and this regulation directly governs creation, maintenance, metadata, retrieval, record series, contractor records, retention, and protection against unauthorized alteration of official records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Official record intake, classification, maintenance, retrieval, retention, and audit trail preservation

authoritative_sources.title: FAR 4.805 - Storage, Handling, and Contract Files
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FAR is the federal procurement rule set. FAR 4.805 directly defines contract-file retention, media conversion, signature preservation, alteration protection, and NARA approval requirements for procurement records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Procurement document completeness, contract-file retention, and award-file handoff

authoritative_sources.title: 2 CFR Part 200 - Uniform Administrative Requirements, Cost Principles, and Audit Requirements for Federal Awards
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of Management and Budget / U.S. Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 2 CFR Part 200 is the authoritative public-program and federal award administration baseline for merit review, public access, records retention, procurement standards, remedies, termination notices, objection procedures, closeout, and audit requirements.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Public-program authority mapping, award or eligibility evidence checks, notice and objection routing, and audit-ready closeout

authoritative_sources.title: 5 U.S.C. 558 - Imposition of Sanctions; Determination of Applications for Licenses
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, U.S. House of Representatives
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This Administrative Procedure Act provision is a primary statute for delegated authority, sanctions, licensing applications, written notice, and opportunity-to-demonstrate-compliance boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Due-process checkpoint detection and official adjudication or enforcement boundary routing
used_for: license, permit, sanction, and notice escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Department of Justice Guide to the Freedom of Information Act
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Justice - Office of Information Policy
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ OIP leads government-wide FOIA policy guidance, making this guide authoritative for agency-record posture, transparency workflows, exemptions, and escalation of release decisions outside routine administration.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Transparency-log, public-records, release, redaction, and access escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Privacy Act of 1974
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Justice - Office of Privacy and Civil Liberties
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ OPCL guidance is authoritative for federal records about individuals, access, amendment, system-of-record, and disclosure constraints that affect policy administration work with case and eligibility records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Privacy, access, amendment, disclosure, and records governance escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Title VI Legal Manual
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Justice - Civil Rights Division
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ Civil Rights Division guidance is authoritative for Title VI nondiscrimination requirements in federally assisted programs, which constrains eligibility, service access, and public-service equity escalation without authorizing this lane to decide civil-rights claims.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Eligibility, public-service equity, and rights-sensitive exception routing

authoritative_sources.title: Standards for Internal Control in the Federal Government - 2025 Green Book
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO's Green Book sets federal internal-control standards and is authoritative for control evidence, documentation, monitoring, deficiency handling, remediation, and auditability expectations in government administration.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Control obligation mapping, evidence review, exception tracking, monitoring, and remediation packaging

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and authority classification
workflow_stages.description: Classify the request, jurisdiction, program, statute or policy source, official record owner, system-of-record dependencies, and whether the work remains administrative before any record or control action.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Evidence and official-record validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate applications, case files, eligibility records, permits, notices, procurement files, correspondence, transparency logs, retention class, and audit trails before status progression.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

workflow_stages.name: Administrative maintenance and control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Prepare record update notes, completeness summaries, administrative status notes, and control-obligation summaries tied to official evidence, owners, review cadence, and due dates.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Retention, access, privacy, and transparency handling
workflow_stages.description: Apply minimum-necessary posture, identify FOIA, Privacy Act, public-records, publication, redaction, amendment, retention, and destruction conflicts, and route official determinations to owners.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

workflow_stages.name: Due-process, equity, adjudication, and enforcement escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when eligibility exceptions, notice, appeal, hearing, license, sanction, public-service equity, procurement award, legal interpretation, or agency authority determines the answer.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception, remediation, and audit-trail closure
workflow_stages.description: Capture unresolved conflicts, missing control evidence, remediation owner, review cadence, due dates, and downstream handoff state without claiming audit opinion or final official signoff.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

artifacts: FOIA, public-records, Privacy Act, and transparency logs
systems_of_record: FOIA, public-records, transparency-log, and disclosure-tracking systems
Audit, control, exception, and remediation tracking systems

allowed: Classify public-sector policy administration requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Validate whether official records, attachments, retention classes, authority sources, and control evidence are complete and traceable for administrative use.
Prepare evidence-backed completeness summaries, administrative status notes, record update notes, retention/access escalation packages, control summaries, and exception or remediation notes.
Map obligations to evidence owners, review cadence, exception owners, remediation state, and downstream routing.
escalate: Eligibility, benefit, permit, license, sanction, appeal, hearing, public-service equity, or public obligation questions require adjudication, legal interpretation, or official authority.
FOIA, public-records, Privacy Act, retention, disclosure, redaction, amendment, publication, or destruction questions require public-records, privacy, or records officer decision.
Procurement award, protest, contract authority, financial approval, or audit opinion is required.
Public baseline and tenant policy conflict on source precedence, retention, access, due process, procurement, or control evidence ownership.
Systems of record are stale, missing, or contradictory.
refuse: Request asks the lane to impersonate official adjudication or enforcement authority.
Request asks the lane to decide legal rights, benefits, procurement outcomes, license status, sanctions, public-records release, Privacy Act amendment, or audit finding closure.
Request asks for unsupported deletion, backdating, concealment, publication, disclosure, destruction, certification, or policy override.
Required tenant facts, official records, or authoritative source context are missing and would change the outcome.

domain_failure_modes: Treating administrative record maintenance as authority to approve, deny, revoke, enforce, disclose, or certify the underlying public-sector outcome.
Proceeding with incomplete applications, missing attachments, stale case files, missing notice evidence, or unverifiable procurement records.
Resolving conflicting official records or policy/source conflicts without an authoritative owner and audit trail.
Ignoring retention, FOIA, public-records, Privacy Act, or tenant access rules when sharing, publishing, redacting, amending, or destroying official records.
Treating public-source baselines as sufficient when tenant jurisdiction, statute, service population, agency delegation, or due-process procedure materially changes the answer.
Handling eligibility exceptions, appeal windows, benefit impacts, Title VI-sensitive concerns, licensing, sanctions, or public obligations as routine administration.
Reporting control compliance when evidence owner, review cadence, monitoring result, exception log, or remediation state is missing.
Using generic compliance language that fails to preserve public-sector terms, artifacts, systems of record, official authority boundaries, and transparency obligations.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing official record or audit trail
Request that implies adjudication or enforcement authority
Required record or evidence package is incomplete
Retention, privacy, or access rule conflicts with the request
Stakeholder asks the lane to approve an official record decision it cannot own
A required control is missing evidence
Unauthorized or unsupported record change request
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence escalation when required facts or authority are missing

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific public-records, privacy, and retention precedence across federal, state, local, tribal, and quasi-public entities
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Tenant must retrieve jurisdiction, agency policy, public-records procedure, privacy rule, and retention schedule before final administrative action. Conflicts route to public records, privacy, or records governance owner.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Program-specific eligibility, appeal, notice, and due-process rules
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Tenant statute, regulation, policy manual, service population, and delegated authority must be retrieved for each program. Rights-sensitive disputes route to program adjudication or legal owner.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Procurement source precedence when local procedures or contract clauses narrow the FAR/public baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Tenant procurement procedure, contract file, solicitation terms, authority delegation, and procurement owner must be retrieved before procurement-adjacent administrative handling.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Control evidence sufficiency for tenant-specific audit or regulator expectations
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Use GAO Green Book as public control baseline and route formal audit opinions, management certifications, or regulator-facing attestations to audit or authorized management owner.

notes: Research output is constrained to government and public-sector policy administration and does not grant legal, adjudicative, enforcement, procurement award, public-records release, privacy determination, or audit-opinion authority.
No blocking ambiguity remains for market-readiness; remaining ambiguities are explicit runtime retrieval and escalation requirements.

Scenario families include evidence gaps, official-record conflicts, retention/access conflicts, rights-sensitive exceptions, adjudication/enforcement refusals, missing control evidence, policy/practice conflict, tenant adaptation, and terminology normalization.
